title: Someone else's words
testCase: Did you wrap the quote in a <blockquote>?
blocks.0.text: Sometimes you quote someone. A review, a line from a film, something a friend said.
blocks.1.text:
You could put it in an ordinary

and add quotation marks. It would look decent. But again you would have drawn a quote instead of saying it is one.
blocks.3.text: The browser indents it — you see that at once. But more importantly, it is now on record: these words are not yours.
blocks.5.text: Search engines tell your opinion from someone else's. If you pass off someone's text as your own, they catch it and it hurts you. Mark it as a quote — and everything is clean.
blocks.6.text: Add a quote to your page. Something someone said about your topic.
